provenance_notes: Codex inspected public base-to-head diffs and authored claim_id, claim_type, claim_text, test_scope, and evidence_items.
cgpro provided decision guidance only; cgpro/provider output is not non-LLM evidence.
Yann has not independently reviewed each selected case after diff inspection, so these records keep human_review_required=true.

deterministic_partition_rule.rule: sort selected case_ids lexicographically; compute sha256('g6d3-holdout:' + case_id); lowest hash becomes holdout, others train

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #378 (ItsDangerous), default SHA-1 digest methods are resolved lazily through `_lazy_sha1` instead of binding `hashlib.sha1` at import/class-definition time. This keeps the default behavior for normal builds while allowing FIPS builds that lack SHA-1 to import and configure alternate digest methods before SHA-1 is touched.
diff_notes: PR pallets/itsdangerous#378 changes src/itsdangerous/signer.py so signer defaults use staticmethod(_lazy_sha1) instead of staticmethod(hashlib.sha1).
tests/test_itsdangerous/test_serializer.py::TestSerializer::test_iter_unsigners imports _lazy_sha1 and asserts the default unsigner uses it before fallback signers use hashlib.sha256.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #689 (sqlite-utils), CLI commands that accept `--functions` can receive the option multiple times and register each inline or file-backed function definition before executing the SQL command.
diff_notes: PR sqlite-utils#689 changes sqlite_utils/cli.py so --functions is multiple=True for relevant CLI commands and values are applied through _register_functions_from_multiple().
tests/test_cli.py::test_query_functions_multiple_invocations invokes two separate --functions definitions and asserts both SQL functions are registered for one query.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #658 (sqlite-utils), `Database.table(name)` is table-only and raises `NoTable` when `name` is a view, while `Database.view(name)` is view-only and raises `NoView` when `name` is not a view. The older `Database.__getitem__` path still dispatches to a view for view names.
diff_notes: PR sqlite-utils#658 changes sqlite_utils/db.py to add NoView, make Database.table() reject view names, add Database.view(), and update table/view helpers to use explicit APIs.
tests/test_create.py::test_bad_table_and_view_exceptions creates one table and one view, then asserts fresh_db.table('v') raises NoTable and fresh_db.view('t') raises NoView.

selected_cases.claim_text: After PR #759 (structlog), `ConsoleRenderer.force_colors` is exposed as a mutable property. Setting it updates `_force_colors`, rebuilds column styles with the current color setting and requested force-colors value, resets level styles to defaults, and reconfigures columns.
diff_notes: PR structlog#759 changes src/structlog/dev.py to store _force_colors and expose a force_colors setter that rebuilds styles, resets level styles, and reconfigures columns.
tests/test_dev.py::TestConsoleRendererForceColorsProperty::test_toggle_force_colors_updates_styles_and_levels toggles force_colors and asserts public/internal state, styles, and level styles update.

deferred_diff_inspected_candidates.reason: Diff primarily drops Python 3.8/3.9 support and modernizes typing syntax; less behaviorally narrow than selected cases.
Removes previously deprecated __version__ fallback without a changed runnable test scope in the diff.
Contains a SQLite pragma compatibility test skip and workflow support changes, but the user-visible behavior claim is weaker than selected sqlite-utils cases.
Public diff includes Rust watcher behavior and Cargo changes; deferred because G-6d.3 stays Python-adapter-focused under current clone-helper boundaries.
Implementation diff is narrow but changed_files_list has no upstream test file; deferred until a narrow runnable upstream test scope can be established without inventing evidence.
Diff is cleanup/refactor-only with no changed runnable test scope.
Narrow deprecation warning diff, but the candidate test is guarded by optional better-exceptions availability outside the standard tests dependency group.
Diff is test-ordering infrastructure rather than a narrow target behavior claim.
Refactor-only terminal initialization diff has no changed runnable test scope.

metadata_reject_groups.reason: broad type/cleanup/resource-management tranche with weaker narrow behavior fit than selected cases
